gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/339)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/339)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَمَّنْ قَالَ وَ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَفِ فَقَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) كَفَّارَتُهُ إِطْعَامُ عَشَرَةِ مَسَاكِينَ مُدّاً مُدّاً مِنْ دَقِيقٍ أَوْ حِنْطَةٍ أَوْ تَحْرِيرِ رَقَبَةٍ أَوْ صِيَامُ ثَلَاثَةِ أَيَّامٍ مُتَوَالِيَاتٍ إِذَا لَمْ يَجِدْ شَيْئاً مِنْ ذَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Al Hakam, from Abu Hamza Al Sumaly who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> from the one who says, ‘By Allah<sup>azwj</sup>’, then does not fulfill it. So Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Its expiation is feeding ten poor, a handful, a handful, from flour or wheat, or freeing of a neck, or Fasting for three days consecutively, when he does not find anything from that’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn al-Hakam from abu Hamzah al-Thumaliy who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who has said by Allah, but has not remained firm about it. He (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, said, ‘The Kaffarah (expiation) for it is to feed ten destitute people, one mud (a certain measurement) to each one of flour or wheat, or free a slave, or fast for three consecutive days if he cannot find the other remedies.’”
